outliers.transcript: I found this great way to make money online and most people don't know it exists. I made over a million dollars working for myself and this method, it's a little uncomfortable. It's not easy, but it's definitely worth it. Here's the play. Have chat GPT interview you on everything you've experienced in life and all the knowledge you've accumulated. Next, tell it to package that into a digital product that's worth $3,000 or more. If you DM me the word prompt, I'll send you the exact script you can use. Then ask it to break down specific topics that will attract the ideal buyers. Now this is the key. Take those topics and turn them into Instagram reels. Instagram will push your videos to the right people if you talk about the same topic consistently. If you don't have followers, it doesn't matter. Instagram guarantees 200 views per video, even if you have zero followers on a brand new account. And this method is not about having a lot of views or a lot of followers. It's about attracting the right focused audience. I grew my account one month from zero followers and already has over 2 million views. I did this by talking about the same topics over and over. Once you start posting, let's say you get a thousand views per video. That's a thousand targeted people looking at what you're talking about. You have a $3,000 digital product that solves a problem that they want. Who's to say that you can't sell one of those people. Sell one of those products every few days. That's already 30k per month. Don't overcomplicate this. It's not that hard.
outliers.why_it_worked: It promised a simple, step-by-step solution to a big problem (making money online) using a popular tool (ChatGPT). The specific numbers and clear math made a complex goal feel tangible and achievable.
outliers.headline: The Anti-Hustle Business Model
outliers.recommended_angle: Frame the method not as a way to make more money, but as a way to build a business that buys back your time. The core value isn't revenue, it's freedom and leverage achieved through AI systems, not content hacks.
my_hooks: I found a way to build a business that runs itself. Most founders are building a prison.
Here's a business model that works better the less you're involved. It's not about content.
I helped brands generate $220M. This is the one system I'd use if I had to start over today.
What if your business grew faster while you were offline? It's possible, but it takes a system.
Stop trading your time for money. Here's how to build an engine that works for you instead.
my_talking_points: I found a way to build an online business that actually buys back your time. And most founders are trapped doing the exact opposite.
I helped generate over $220M for 117 brands. I saw the same pattern everywhere. The founder becomes the bottleneck. The business owns them.
There's a fix, but it requires some upfront work. You have to build the engine once. But it is worth it.
Here's the play. First, you identify the three most repetitive tasks in your business. Think lead follow up, client onboarding, or answering the same questions over and over.
Next, you install an AI employee to run those tasks for you. This is not a chatbot. It's a system that works in your voice, 24/7, while you're hiking or with your family.
That one system can save you 10 to 15 hours a week. That's a full work week back every single month.
It does the work of a part-time hire without the salary or the management.
This isn't about getting more views or followers. It's about creating leverage. It's about building a business that serves you, not the other way around.
I put together a guide on the three AI employees every business needs. If you want it, just comment the word 'FieldWork' below and I'll send it over.

outliers.transcript: I was at Alex Ormosi's house the other day, and he called my YouTube channel The Naked Funnel, but it signs a $30,000 client every 16 hours. Let me sketch it out. Every YouTube video I post has a job. Its job is to deliver amazing value and invite people to watch a particular vid. It's called The Million Dollar Plan. So every one of these videos, at the end, invites people to see the plan. And I built it to answer one question, what's the one piece of content that I wish every prospect saw before they even thought about working with us? At the end of the video, people who want to find out about working with us, message me a secret code word on Instagram. I'm not going to tell you the code word now, we'll just call it secret, because it is. And that triggers a really simple ManyChat automation, just to work out, are they qualified, or are they not? And if they are, it sends them our offer doc. And at the end, if it's a match, they become a happy client, paying money. Every 16 hours, somebody goes to this step without ever talking to anybody. So what did Alex like about it, and why does he call it Naked Funnel? Well, I think he dug it, because most people build very complicated funnels on elaborate pages, which are all trying to sell things to you. And this isn't trying to sell, it's trying to sort. Here's the cool part. I set this up when the channel was tiny, and I was getting like a couple of hundred views on each video. And this sales video was unlisted. The only way to find it was through content. Binges are buyers. You don't need a complicated funnel, and you don't need a big audience. You can make big sales from a small channel. So that's the Naked Funnel. It's really easy to set up when you know how, and I'm getting a few coaches together to do exactly that on a live workshop. If you want to turn your show into real business, just comment showbiz below, and I'll give you the invite with all the dates.
outliers.why_it_worked: It combined a powerful authority hook (a story involving Alex Hormozi) with a specific, desirable result ($30k client every 16 hours) and then demystified the exact process, making it seem achievable.
